CREATE TABLE test_list (id INTEGER, values ARRAY<INTEGER>);
SELECT id, array_indexof(values, 2) AS index_of_2 FROM test_list;
SELECT id, array_indexof(values, 4) AS index_of_4 FROM test_list WHERE id != 2;
SELECT id, array_indexof(values, NULL) AS index_of_NULL FROM test_list;
SELECT id, array_indexof(values, 1) AS index_of_1 FROM test_list WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_list WHERE id > 1);
SELECT tl.id, array_indexof(tl.values, tv.value) AS index FROM test_list tl JOIN test_values tv ON array_indexof(tl.values, tv.value) IS NOT NULL;
WITH data AS (SELECT id, array_indexof(values, 3) AS idx FROM test_list) SELECT id FROM data WHERE idx IS NOT NULL;
SELECT at.value, array_indexof(at.value, 1) AS index_of_1 FROM another_test at WHERE array_indexof(at.value, 10) IS NULL;
SELECT id, array_indexof(values, 7) AS index_of_7 FROM test_list WHERE array_indexof(values, 9) IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE mixed_lists (id INTEGER, mixed_array ARRAY<INTEGER>);
SELECT id, array_indexof(mixed_array, NULL) AS index_of_NULL FROM mixed_lists WHERE array_indexof(mixed_array, 1) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT ARRAY_AGG(id) AS list_ids, array_indexof(array_agg(values), 2) AS index_of_2 FROM test_list GROUP BY id;
